Article: EOE Credit Sequence
[]: EOE Credit Sequence             Quote Post by Machinehead » Sat Aug 20, 2005 5:22 pm In the American release of the EOE DVD, the music on the credits is less than spectacular. I mean, it's alright, but it could have been a good track. Personally, I would have liked to seen a slow, and long credit sequence of single credits fading in and out to a white background. I would have wanted the music to be 'Pachebel Cannon', the same music used at the end of Death. What about you guys?

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Sat Aug 20, 2005 5:24 pm do you mean the dna spirally thingy... i dont much care for that scene i skip it lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Machinehead » Sat Aug 20, 2005 5:26 pm TheBlueTree wrote: do you mean the dna spirally thingy... i dont much care for that scene i skip it lol.

[]:             Quote Post by K2Grey » Sun Aug 21, 2005 11:22 am End it the same way as it ended in Japan - credits during the intermission, at end blank white screen, then blank black screen, then confused viewers :)

[]:             Quote Post by Machinehead » Sun Aug 21, 2005 12:38 pm K2Grey wrote: End it the same way as it ended in Japan - credits during the intermission, at end blank white screen, then blank black screen, then confused viewers :)

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Aug 22, 2005 1:03 am Even a twenty-second pause before commencing the Mangle creds woud have shown better taste. And for the love of fuck, some SILENCE! :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon Aug 22, 2005 1:45 pm K2Grey wrote: End it the same way as it ended in Japan - credits during the intermission, at end blank white screen, then blank black screen, then confused viewers :)

[]:             Quote Post by Oboe » Mon Aug 22, 2005 5:18 pm Or you could end it like the video release and have simple white credits on a

Article: EOE Credit Sequence
 blank black screen, then confused viewers :)

[]:             Quote Post by Oboe » Mon Aug 22, 2005 5:18 pm Or you could end it like the video release and have simple white credits on a black background scroll to Jesu Joy of Man's Desiring. That's my favorite version.

[]:             Quote Post by K2Grey » Mon Aug 22, 2005 9:23 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: K2Grey wrote: End it the same way as it ended in Japan - credits during the intermission, at end blank white screen, then blank black screen, then confused viewers :)

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Tue Aug 23, 2005 12:42 am well, what can you expect from the need to translate the credits, and bandai-style so as not to sacrifice the almighty dna credits roll? (to be fair, bandai's release of "infinite ryvius" has ABSOLUTELY NOTHING playing over the translated credits at the end of each dvd for some bizarre reason, but that's another story.) i agree that "jesu, joy of man's desiring" would have been a better choice for the translated credits... the piano version, i mean. or they could have just included them as part of the extras or something. ah well, it wouldn't be mangle if it didn't have less-than-stellar release quality. r_r
